task_objective.description: Design a social simulator to generate daily mobility trajectories of urban residents under an out-of-distribution (OOD) setting (N2A: Normal-to-Abnormal). A trajectory is an ordered sequence of (location, time) visits: {(l0,t0),(l1,t1),...,(ln,tn)}. In this task, the simulator MUST be trained/fitted only on normal-period records (2019-2020), while model selection / calibration MUST use abnormal-period targets (2021) as validation. Final performance MUST be reported on a held-out 2021 test split.
task_objective.simulation_focus:
Goal: generate one-day trajectories per resident for abnormal-period target days in 2021 (LLM is optional).
Key latent factors: Pattern (habitual routines), Persona (semantic role), Motivation (day-specific intent).
Pipeline (Online OOD):
(1) From normal historical trajectories (2019-2020 ONLY), derive Pattern (NL summary of routines/statistics) via LLM (optional).
(2) From normal historical behavior (2019-2020 ONLY), infer Persona (e.g., office worker/student/night-shift) via LLM (optional).
(3) For a 2021 target date, summarize Motivation from the resident’s past 7 available days BEFORE the target date. These past-7 days MAY include 2021 days strictly earlier than the target date (Online OOD), and MAY backfill from late 2020 if early-2021 history is insufficient.
(4) Prompt LLM with {Pattern, Persona, Motivation} to output a target-day trajectory string in the SAME format as 1921Y.json (no rationale).
task_objective.Data Calibration Procedure:
Calibration / model selection is performed on abnormal-period targets (2021) with a two-level split:
(0) Split 2021 targets into two disjoint subsets per resident (or globally by date):
    - 2021-calib (V_calib): used for calibration, hyper-parameter selection, best_params selection, and early stopping.
    - 2021-test (V_test): NEVER used for any calibration/selection; used ONLY once for final report.
(1) Fit all priors/Pattern/Persona using ONLY 2019-2020 data (no 2021 usage in fitting).
(2) For each calibration iteration, evaluate candidate parameters/prompts on 2021-calib targets ONLY:
    - For each 2021-calib target day, build Motivation from the past-7-day window strictly before the target day (Online OOD: may include earlier 2021 days; no future leakage).
    - Predict the target-day trajectory.
    - Compare with 2021-calib ground truth and compute metrics; select best_params and apply early stopping based on these metrics.
(3) After best_params is chosen, run ONE final evaluation on 2021-test targets and report final metrics. 2021-test must not influence any choice.
task_objective.Simulator Design Requirements:
Build a simulator that generates daily trajectories.

CRITICAL SCOPE CONSTRAINT (STRICT) — N2A + Online OOD:
- Training / fitting / building priors / Pattern / Persona MUST ONLY use 1921Y.json records whose dates fall in 2019-01-01 to 2020-12-31 (inclusive).
- Validation targets for calibration/model selection MUST ONLY be days whose dates fall in 2021-01-01 to 2021-12-31 (inclusive).
- 2021 MUST be split into 2021-calib (used for calibration / selecting best_params / early stop) and 2021-test (held-out final report only).
- Online OOD input window rule: Motivation / short-term context for a 2021 target day MAY use the resident’s observed days in 2021 that are STRICTLY earlier than the target day; it MAY also backfill from late 2020 when needed. It MUST NEVER use any 2021 day on/after the target day (no peeking into the future).
- Optimization signals (metrics for choosing best_params, early stopping) MUST come ONLY from 2021-calib. 2021-test must never be used for any selection.

Must support:
1) Data loading: load residents' daily trajectories from 1921Y.json; construct three disjoint subsets:
   (a) TRAIN_NORMAL: 2019-2020 only (for fitting priors/Pattern/Persona).
   (b) V_CALIB_ABNORMAL: 2021-calib targets only (for calibration, best_params selection, early stop).
   (c) V_TEST_ABNORMAL: 2021-test targets only (for final report only).
2) Pattern & Persona init: for each resident, generate/store Pattern + Persona using ONLY TRAIN_NORMAL (2019-2020).
3) 2021 split (calib/test): create V_CALIB_ABNORMAL and V_TEST_ABNORMAL by temporal split on 2021 dates (recommended: early part of 2021 for calib, later part for test) or another deterministic split. Ensure no overlap.
4) Online OOD Prediction (2021): for each 2021 target day (calib or test), compute Motivation from the past 7 available days strictly before target date (may include earlier 2021 days; may backfill from late 2020). Prompt LLM (optional) with {Pattern, Persona, Motivation}; output a trajectory string in the SAME STRING FORMAT as 1921Y.json (no rationale); evaluate against 2021 ground truth.
5) Strict separation: (a) TRAIN_NORMAL is 2019-2020 only; (b) calibration/selection uses ONLY V_CALIB_ABNORMAL; (c) final report uses ONLY V_TEST_ABNORMAL; (d) no future leakage for Motivation windows.

LLM output hard constraint (STRICT):
- Output MUST be a single string that exactly follows the 1921Y.json day-record format:
  "Activities at YYYY-MM-DD: POI#id at HH:MM:SS, POI#id at HH:MM:SS, ... ."
- Stops are comma-separated; preserve "POI#id" and HH:MM:SS; end with a period.
- Do NOT include rationale/reasons, extra fields, markdown, or JSON.
task_objective.Experimental settings:
ONLY ONE setting is allowed:
- N2A Online OOD: 2019/2020 (Normal Train/Fit) -> 2021 (Abnormal Validation Targets).

Rules:
- Calibration/selection targets: 2021-calib only.
- Final report targets: 2021-test only.

DISALLOWED:
- Using 2021 in training/fitting priors/Pattern/Persona.
- Using 2021-test for calibration, best_params selection, or early stopping.
- Using any 2021 day on/after a target day as input context for that target day.
task_objective.Input-Output Mapping of the Simulated Ag:
Input: (i) resident long-term priors/Pattern/Persona fitted ONLY from 2019-2020; (ii) for a 2021 target date, Motivation/short-term context is summarized using the past 7 available days strictly before the target date (Online OOD: may include earlier 2021; may backfill from late 2020; never use future days); optional contextual cue for the target date (can be blank).
Output: a single trajectory string in the same format as 1921Y.json:
"Activities at YYYY-MM-DD: POI#id at HH:MM:SS, POI#id at HH:MM:SS, ... ." (no rationale/reasons; no JSON).
Evaluation: compute metrics on 2021-calib for calibration/selection and on 2021-test for final report. Never mix them.

data_files.1921Y.json:
Resident daily trajectories across multiple years. For THIS TASK (N2A Online OOD), split by date into three disjoint subsets:
(A) TRAIN_NORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2019-01-01, 2020-12-31]. Used for training/fitting priors/Pattern/Persona ONLY.
(B) V_CALIB_ABNORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2021-01-01, 2021-12-31] AND assigned to the 2021-calib split. Used for calibration/model selection (best_params, early stop) ONLY.
(C) V_TEST_ABNORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2021-01-01, 2021-12-31] AND assigned to the 2021-test split. Used ONLY once for final report.

Format:
- Key: resident_id (e.g., "1874").
- Value: list of day strings, each begins with "Activities at YYYY-MM-DD:" followed by comma-separated stops.
- Stop unit: "POI#id at HH:MM:SS" (arrival time at that POI).

STRICT RULES:
- 2019-2020 data MUST NOT include any 2021 records.
- 2021 MUST be split into 2021-calib and 2021-test; they must be disjoint.
- Calibration/selection MUST use ONLY 2021-calib targets.
- Final reporting MUST use ONLY 2021-test targets.
- Online OOD input window: when predicting a 2021 target day, Motivation/short-term context MAY use the resident’s observed days strictly before the target day (including earlier 2021 days and optionally late-2020 backfill). It MUST NEVER use target-day or future 2021 days.

Linking:
- Category lookup: strip suffix "#id" from "POI#id" -> "POI"; map "POI" to category via catto.json.
- Coordinates lookup: use full "POI#id" to retrieve (lat, lon) from poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude_complement_alignment_clean.json.

Output format is strict; follow the exact tokens and separators shown above.
data_files.poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.j: Maps each full POI name (e.g., "Rest Area#1061") to its latitude/longitude. Use it to get coordinates for each visited stop (for both 2019-2020 fitting and 2021 evaluation).
data_files.catto.json: Maps base POI name (suffix removed; e.g., "Rest Area") to a semantic category (e.g., "Travel & Transport"). Use it to compute category-based metrics.

category_share_mae.description: Mean absolute error between the generated and ground-truth distributions of visit category shares (computed from stop categories mapped via catto.json) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

stop_count_abs_mean_error.description: Absolute mean error of the number of stops per day between generated and ground-truth trajectories on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

stop_count_kl.description: Kullback-Leibler divergence between the generated and ground-truth distributions of daily stop counts on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

tod_jsd_avg.description: Average Jensen-Shannon divergence between generated and ground-truth time-of-day (ToD) distributions of stop arrival times on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

topk_poi_recall.description: Recall@K of top-K most frequently visited POIs: fraction of ground-truth top-K POIs that appear in generated trajectories (aggregated over the evaluated target set). Higher is better.

transition_divergence.description: Divergence between generated and ground-truth transition behavior (e.g., POI-to-POI or category-to-category transition distributions) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

trip_distance_wasserstein.description: Wasserstein distance between generated and ground-truth trip distance distributions (computed from consecutive-stop geographic distances using poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.
